recipes.name: 2,4-D Amine Standard
recipes.notes: Apply at rosette stage. Cheap and effective for broad-acre.

recipes.name: Metsulfuron + 2,4-D Combo
recipes.notes: Dual action — 2,4-D for knockdown, metsulfuron for residual seedling suppression.

recipes.name: Metsulfuron Solo (Residual)
recipes.notes: Good residual control. Slower acting but suppresses seedling germination for months.

recipes.name: Garlon 600 Aerial Foliar
recipes.notes: Standard foliar for rubber vine. Full leaf coverage critical.

recipes.name: Imazapyr + Triclopyr Combo
recipes.notes: Dual action — triclopyr for foliar knockdown, imazapyr for soil residual preventing regrowth.

recipes.name: Graslan Pellets (Granular)
recipes.notes: Soil-active tebuthiuron. Apply before wet season. Broad-spectrum — affects all woody species.

recipes.name: 2,4-D Amine Standard
recipes.notes: Apply at rosette stage in autumn-winter. Cheap and effective for large areas.

recipes.name: MCPA + Metsulfuron Combo
recipes.notes: MCPA for knockdown, metsulfuron for residual. Good for paddocks with mixed broadleaf weeds.

recipes.name: Metsulfuron Solo (Residual)
recipes.notes: Good residual but slower. Suppresses seedling germination. Best where ongoing management needed.

weed: Scotch thistle / spear thistle / variegated thistle

recipes.name: 2,4-D Amine Standard
recipes.notes: Rosette stage ONLY. Once thistles bolt, 2,4-D is largely ineffective.

recipes.name: Clopyralid (Lontrel) — Premium Option
recipes.notes: Highly selective Asteraceae killer. Excellent residual. Safe in grass. Higher cost but best efficacy.

recipes.name: MCPA + Clopyralid Combo
recipes.notes: Broadens the broadleaf spectrum while maintaining clopyralid residual on thistles.

recipes.name: Glyphosate Standard Knockdown
recipes.notes: Standard pre-plant fallow knockdown. Non-selective, no residual. Cheapest option.

recipes.name: Glyphosate + 2,4-D Double Knock
recipes.notes: Industry standard double-knock for resistant weed management. 2,4-D adds broadleaf residual. Check plant-back periods.

recipes.name: Glyphosate + Dicamba (Tough Broadleaf)
recipes.adjuvant: Per label only — NO extra oils
recipes.notes: For fallow with tough broadleaf weeds (marshmallow, fleabane). EXTREME drift caution with dicamba — temp and wind restrictions.

recipes.name: Taskforce 745 Standard
recipes.notes: Same approach as GRT grass. Flupropanate is the primary tool.

recipes.name: Taskforce + Glyphosate Knockdown
recipes.adjuvant: NO surfactant with Taskforce — self-surfactant glyphosate only
recipes.notes: Glyphosate for immediate visual knockdown, Taskforce for long-term residual.

recipes.name: Taskforce High Rate
recipes.notes: For heavy infestations. Extended withholding at higher rates.

recipes.name: Taskforce 745 Standard
recipes.notes: Flupropanate is the only effective chemical for Coolatai grass.

recipes.name: Taskforce + Glyphosate
recipes.notes: Glyphosate knockdown + flupropanate residual. Plan pasture re-seeding.

recipes.name: Taskforce High Rate (Dense)
recipes.notes: For dense Coolatai infestations. Monitor and re-treat as needed.
